title: PersonaConfig
description: Static configuration that resolves a Playwright Persona to the synthetic identity surfaced under mock-auth mode. One entry per persona, encoded in web/tests/auth/personas.ts. Mock auth replaced the original spec-007 real-Entra approach (see research §R11 — pivot rationale).

persona.description: The persona this config applies to.

mockAccount.description: Synthetic identity surfaced under mock-auth mode. Populates the mock MSAL AccountInfo and the backend mock principal claims. None of these values resolve against any real directory — they exist to give the SPA something to render and to make log correlation deterministic.

oid.description: Stable per-persona GUID. NOT a real Entra object ID. Hardcoded so trace correlation across runs is deterministic.

upn.description: Synthetic UPN. Domain segment `mock.busterminal.dev` is reserved and never resolved — makes the synthetic origin obvious in logs.

displayName.description: Human-friendly display name surfaced in the user menu and the no-access page.

expectedRoleAssignments.description: The set of BusTerminal.* roles the persona's mock principal carries. The frontend api-client serialises this list into the `X-Mock-Roles` request header; the backend `MockAuthenticationHandler` translates it into ClaimsPrincipal Role claims. Empty array for the `none` persona (authenticated but role-empty).

storageStatePath.description: Filesystem path of the persona's persisted Playwright storageState, RELATIVE TO THE WEB PACKAGE ROOT (web/). Kept as a future hook for any spec that wants to opt into a captured storageState (none do today under mock auth). The mock-auth fixture does not write this file; it relies on `addInitScript` per-context instead.
